root_text.an6.112:1.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya tayo dhammā bhāvetabbā.
root_text.an6.112:1.7: Assādadiṭṭhiyā pahānāya aniccasaññā bhāvetabbā, attānudiṭṭhiyā pahānāya anattasaññā bhāvetabbā, micchādiṭṭhiyā pahānāya sammādiṭṭhi bhāvetabbā.
root_text.an6.112:1.8: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya ime tayo dhammā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.an6.112:1.3: The view that things are gratifying, the theory of self, and wrong view.
translation_text.an6.112:1.5: To give up these three things you should develop three things.
translation_text.an6.112:1.7: You should develop the perception of impermanence to give up the view that things are gratifying; the perception of not-self to give up the theory of self; and right view to give up wrong view.
translation_text.an6.112:1.8: These are the three things you should develop to give up those three things.”
